memoryArtifacts.headline: Mayflower Families Through Five Generations Vol 23 Part I John Howland part 2 of 4
memoryArtifacts.description: If you are related to John Howland and Elizabeth Tilley then you are also related to three other Mayflower Pilgrims, John and Mrs. John Tilley and their daughter, Elizabeth Tilley.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lieutenant James Brown Jr.於1655年5月4日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯時，他的父親James Brown32歲，母親Lydia Howland22歲。他於1678年6月5日與Margaret Denison在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1718年5月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托阿特萊包洛享年63歲，埋葬在美國羅得島普洛維登斯易亞斯特 普洛維登斯安希特 蕾托 內可 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lieutenant James Brown Jr.于1655年5月4日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯时，他的父亲James Brown32岁，母亲Lydia Howland22岁。他于1678年6月5日与Margaret Denison在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1718年5月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托阿特萊包洛享年63岁，埋葬在美国罗德岛州普洛維登斯易亞斯特 普洛維登斯安希特 蕾托 內可 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Brown
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Brunson
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Brun (Old English Brūna or Old Norse Brúni) + -son ‘Brown's son’. See Brown . There may also be occasional survivals of an Old English compound personal name Brūnsunu ‘brown son’, attested in the Domesday Book, and presumably given to a child with brown hair or complexion. Whether it was also used as an adult nickname is uncertain. This form of the surname is most common in Norfolk.

variants.name: Browning
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Brouning (Old English Brūning), a derivative of the byname Brūn (see Brown ).

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. William Browning was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Browne
meanings: English: variant of   Brown  .
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Burnett
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Tyrone and Antrim): descriptive nickname of Norman origin, from Old French burnete, a diminutive of brun ‘brown’ (see Brown ), or possibly in some instances a metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of burnete, a high quality woolen cloth, originally dark brown in color.
Probably also an altered form of English   Barnett  .
In some cases also an altered form of French   Burnet  ; compare   Burnette  .

variants.name: Erjavec
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from an old spelling of rjavec, most commonly meaning ‘dark skinned man’ and ‘brown horse’. Compare Brown .

variants.name: Borron
meanings: Scottish: perhaps a variant of   Brown   in a Scottish pronunciation. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Broun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Lyubov, Sergey.
Scottish: variant of   Brown  .
Jewish: variant of   Braun  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Lieutenant James Brown Jr. was born on 4 May 1655, in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, his father, James Brown, was 32 and his mother, Lydia Howland, was 22. He married Margaret Denison on 5 June 1678, in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 15 May 1718, in Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 63, and was buried in Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lieutenant James Brown Jr. est né le 4 mai 1655, à Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, James Brown, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lydia Howland, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Denison le 5 juin 1678, à Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 15 mai 1718, à Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lieutenant James Brown Jr. am 4. Mai 1655 in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Brown 32 und seine Mutter Lydia Howland 22. Er hat am 5. Juni 1678 in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Margaret Denison geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 15. Mai 1718 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lieutenant James Brown Jr. è nato il il 4 maggio 1655 nel Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, James Brown, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Lydia Howland, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Denison il 5 giugno 1678, nel Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 15 maggio 1718, nel Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Lieutenant James Brown Jr.が1655年5月4日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーブリストルレhオボスで生まれた時，父James Brownは32歳，母Lydia Howlandは22歳であった。1678年6月5日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルスワンセアでMargaret Denisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1718年5月15日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルアットレボロで63歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ロードアイランド州プロヴィデンスエアスト プロヴィデンスアンシエント リットル ネック セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lieutenant James Brown Jr.(이)가 1655년 5월 4일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Brown(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Lydia Howland(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1678년 6월 5일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 스완시에서 Margaret Denison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1718년 5월 15일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 애틀레버로에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 로드아일랜드 주 프로비데스 이스트 프로비데스 앤시트 리틀 니c크 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lieutenant James Brown Jr. nasceu em 4 de maio de 1655, em Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, James Brown, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lydia Howland, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Denison em 5 de junho de 1678, em Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 15 de maio de 1718, em Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lieutenant James Brown Jr. родился 4 мая 1655 г. в местности рэобот, бристол, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени James Brown было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lydia Howland, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Denison 5 июня 1678 г. в местности свансеа, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 15 мая 1718 г. в местности аттлеборо, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности ансиент литл некк кеметири, эаст провиденс, провиденс, Род-Айленд, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lieutenant James Brown Jr. nació el 4 de mayo de 1655, en Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, James Brown, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lydia Howland, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Margaret Denison el 5 de junio de 1678, en Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1718, en Attleboro, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Ancient Little Neck Cemetery, East Providence, Providence, Rhode Island, Estados Unidos.
